exercises.note: Selecting one favorite from all possible colors calls for cuál — and since cuál isn't glued directly onto a noun here, the Castellano regional exception doesn't apply.

exercises.prompt: (Holding up an unfamiliar object) ¿___ es esto?
exercises.note: Asking for a definition or identification of an unknown thing calls for qué, not cuál.
This is an indirect question — qué keeps its accent even though there's no question mark. Sé (I know) also needs its own accent to distinguish it from se, the reflexive/impersonal pronoun.

exercises.incorrectSentence: No vine por qué estaba enfermo.
exercises.note: Por qué (why, two words, accented) only introduces a question. The connector 'because' is porque, one word, no accent.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in order to form a question:
Match each interrogative word to the kind of question it asks:

pairs.right: asks for a definition or identification
asks to select from a set

exercises.prompt: Fill in each blank with the correct interrogative word:
exercises.passage: —¿{0} te llamas? —Me llamo Marta. —¿{1} vives? —Vivo en Madrid. —No sé {2} hora es, pero deberíamos irnos.

exercises.prompt: Embed this question as an indirect question after 'No sé':
exercises.instruction: embed as an indirect question after No sé
